; confirm vaccination records on file. 2.2. No known medical conditions or medications; monitor as usual for: - Changes in appetite, stool, urination, or energy level. - Coughing, sneezing, limping, or visible discomfort. - Signs of stress: excessive panting, pacing, whining, or hiding. 2.3. If any abnormal signs persist beyond one routine check or worsen quickly: - Notify supervisor immediately. - If urgent, follow facility emergency protocol (contact vet, then owner).
3.1. Assign Charlie a clean, appropriately sized kennel or suite. 3.2. Provide: - Clean, non‑slip bedding. - Fresh water bowl (changed at every meal and at least 2x additional times daily). -

placed in kennel for comfort and sleep. 3.3. Keep kennel area calm and free from excessive noise when possible. 3.4. Do not overcrowd his kennel with extra toys; limit to his favorite toy plus 1–2 safe facility toys if desired.

for eating; remove any uneaten food, record if he eats less than 75% of normal. 4.4.

Walking procedure: - Use secure collar/harness and leash; check fit before exiting kennel. - Walk using designated potty/exercise areas only. - Allow time for sniffing and elimination; note stools and urine in daily log. 6.3. Adjust intensity if: - Hot weather – shorten walk, provide shade, watch for overheating. - Cold/wet weather – limit exposure as needed, dry him off afterward. 6.4. Do not run or bike with Charlie unless specifically authorized by facility and owner.
and is generally friendly, but excitement must be managed. 7.2. Before group play, follow facility temperament and introduction protocol: - Start with

through a barrier or in neutral space. - Observe for signs of over‑arousal: jumping, vocalizing, intense pulling. - If overly excited, have him sit and focus before allowing closer interaction. 7.3. Group play guidelines (if he passes temperament check and is cleared by supervisor): - Place him with
by staff. - Limit initial group sessions to
, extend only if calm and appropriate. 7.4. Signs to

socialization: - Mounting, chasing that doesn’t stop when the other dog signals. - Excessive barking, growling, or stiff body posture. - Any sign of fear or distress in Charlie or other dogs. 7.5. If he becomes too excited: - Call him away using cheerful voice. - Ask for a sit, reward with
. - Provide a brief timeout in a quiet area if needed.
; make fetch a primary enrichment activity. 8.2.

- Use a secure, fenced area only. - 1–2 fetch sessions per day, 10–15 minutes each, depending on weather and his energy level. - Use facility‑approved balls/toys; do not throw sticks or unsafe items. 8.3. Play rules: - Ask for a simple behavior (sit or “wait”) before throwing the ball. - Encourage calm returns; if he becomes overly amped, pause until he is calmer. 8.4. Additional enrichment options (as time allows): - Short training games (sit, down, stay, recall) using praise and belly rubs. - Gentle brushing if he enjoys being groomed. - Quiet one‑on‑one time in a calm room with petting and belly rubs. 8.5. Avoid: - Rough play or wrestling. - Tug with toys that could encourage resource guarding unless staff is trained and policy allows.
9.1. Use calm, confident handling at all times. 9.2.

belly rubs and gentle physical affection. 9.3. When greeting or rewarding Charlie: - Invite him to sit or stand calmly. - Offer chest or shoulder petting first, then belly rubs when he rolls over. 9.4. Avoid: - Looming directly over his face or hugging tightly. - Rough pats; keep touch gentle and controlled. 9.5. If he appears anxious (pacing, whining, panting): - Offer quiet interaction, belly rubs, and a short walk if appropriate. - Ensure his blue squeaky toy is with him for comfort.
- Always double‑check kennel doors, gates, and exterior doors are latched.
opening kennel door; hold leash securely when entering/exiting runs.
outside of secure, fenced areas approved for off‑leash play.
10.3. Use only facility‑approved collars/harnesses; no slip leads as regular collars unless policy allows and staff is trained.
10.4. Never leave Charlie unattended with other dogs or with toys that could be swallowed or destroyed easily.
10.5. In case of a dog scuffle:
- Do not grab collars near mouths.
- Use approved interruption methods (loud noise, barrier, water hose as per facility protocol).
- Separate dogs calmly, check for injuries, notify supervisor and complete incident report.
11.1. Check Charlie at each shift for:
- Remove waste promptly from kennel and play areas.
- Disinfect food and water bowls daily and after each boarding turnover.
- In hot weather: limit intense fetch, provide shade and water breaks every 5–10 minutes.
- In cold/wet weather: limit outdoor time, towel dry after walks.
11.4. If Charlie shows any sign of heat stress (excessive panting, drooling, weakness):
- Move him to a cool area immediately.
- Notify supervisor and follow medical emergency protocol.
- Confirm he had dinner and his
- Confirm he had an evening potty break/walk.
12.2. Keep lights and noise low in kennel area after designated “lights out” time.
- Briefly check for medical or potty needs.
- Provide calm reassurance; avoid extended stimulation or play.
13.1. At each shift, update Charlie’s log with:
- Socialization and play activities (including fetch sessions).
- Any unusual behavior, signs of stress, or possible health issues.
13.2. Report significant changes or concerns to supervisor and note any instructions.
13.3. If any emergency or serious concern arises:
- Ensure Charlie is clean (light brushing if needed).
14.2. Provide a brief behavior and care summary to front desk:
- Socialization and play preferences (highlight love of fetch and belly rubs).
Staff should follow this checklist consistently to ensure Charlie’s boarding stay is safe, comfortable, and enriched.
